Useful recent lineup reference in a competitive away game.

notes: Current phase is team_profile only; no score, group, champion, or betting output.
fan_summary_en: Czechia are the kind of team that can make a match feel old-fashioned in the most dangerous way: crosses, second balls, Soucek chaos, Schick finishing, and enough structure to keep you uncomfortable for 90 minutes.

sample_summary.primary_window: 2025-2026 European qualifying sample and current World Cup squad context
notes: Uses FIFA and UEFA official material plus the local data packet.
A broader event-level sample would sharpen the exact balance between back-three and back-four usage.

coach_profile.summary: Hasek publicly talks about positive football, but in tournament reality Czechia still project as a physically assertive, structure-first side. They can alter the nominal shape, yet the core identity remains direct service, second-ball aggression, and strong central duel presence.

team_shape.in_possession_shape: Usually built to feed a strong central striker, attack from crossing areas, and keep enough midfield or wingback presence for second phases.
team_shape.out_of_possession_shape: Can defend in a compact mid/low block with strong central duel orientation, whether from a back three or a flatter back four.
role_asymmetry: Schick is the focal point rather than one interchangeable attacker among many.
Soucek's late-box timing and aerial role distort the midfield shape in attacking phases.

key_roles.note: The clearest single-point attacking reference, combining penalty-box finishing with enough link presence to hold direct service.
Essential for aerial power, second balls, and late-arriving central damage.
This layer determines whether Czechia can create variety beyond early service into Schick and Soucek.
Wide delivery and defensive width management are central to the Czechia game model.

attack.summary: Czechia's attack is built on Schick finishing, Soucek's secondary threat, and repeated wide service. They are dangerous without needing huge possession shares.
notes: When Schick is well supplied, Czechia can create good-value box pressure quickly.
Set pieces and restarts are not secondary decorations; they are core tactical weapons.

defense.summary: Czechia defend with physical central presence and a willingness to sit in a disciplined block. Their biggest problems appear when quick opponents stretch them wide or force repeated recovery sprints.
notes: The defensive model is more about structure and duel winning than all-phase front-foot pressing.
Fast wide overloads can pull Czechia into less comfortable defensive races.

set_pieces.attack: One of Czechia's clearest strengths, driven by size, timing, and delivery into crowded central zones.
set_pieces.defense: Generally reliable in aerial terms, though the exact full match-state mapping of markers and targets still needs expansion.

coach_tendencies.leading_adjustments: When ahead, Czechia are likely to protect central duels, preserve aerial security, and continue to value territory and service rather than chasing stylistic dominance.
coach_tendencies.trailing_adjustments: When trailing, they can increase crossing volume, commit more bodies around Schick and Soucek, and lean even harder on second phases and set pieces.
coach_tendencies.summary: Hasek may speak about positive football, but the current competitive picture still points to a team more comfortable making matches rugged, direct, and physically loaded than turning them into open technical contests.

attack_ceiling.note: Good enough to punish teams that lose box control, but less explosive than top transition-heavy sides.

defensive_floor.note: Structure and physicality help, but pace stress can lower the floor.

tempo_preference.note: Czechia are comfortable in stoppage-heavy, duel-heavy scripts rather than constant speed.

transition_attack.note: Useful, but not the team's clearest identity edge.

transition_defense_risk.note: The risk rises when wide spaces open and repeated recovery runs are required.

key_player_dependency.note: Schick and Soucek shape the entire game model more than most star pairs on comparable teams.

market_relevant_flags.note: He is the central attacking reference that organizes the rest of the chance profile.
His late runs and aerial impact are central to second-phase danger.

market_relevant_flags.factor: whether the opponent can stretch Czechia wide
market_relevant_flags.note: Wide speed and overload pressure can lower Czechia's defensive comfort.

verification_questions.claim: Czechia's core identity remains direct service, second balls, and set-piece pressure regardless of nominal formation.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Needs a larger tactical sample to separate shape variation from identity variation.

verification_questions.claim: Schick and Soucek are the two main structural hinges of the whole attack.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Should be tested against matches where the creative layer carried more of the burden.

verification_questions.claim: Fast wide attacks are the most reliable way to stress Czechia defensively.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Needs video-backed transition and wing-defense review across a wider sample.

data.completion_note: Deep version completed to a usable handoff level, but packet remains partial pending full lineup-linked sample expansion and a complete set-piece map.
